message:
feat(core): CTE support for UPDATE/DELETE, collation-aware DISTINCT, VDBE safety, and broad correctness fixes

Major feature additions, SQLite behavioral conformance improvements, and
safety hardening across the entire stack.

CTE & Query Support:
- Add execute_delete_with_ctes() and execute_update_with_ctes() with
  materialize_with_clause()/cleanup_cte_tables() helpers for WITH clause
  support in DML statements
- Add close_in_place(&mut self) for inspectable/retriable close semantics

Database Validation Infrastructure:
- Add pragma_integrity_check_rows(), validate_database_integrity(),
  validate_sqlite_master_rows(), validate_schema_btrees_in_txn() and
  supporting helpers (parse_database_header_checked, sqlite_master_signature,
  is_implicit_autoindex_name, is_virtual_table_sql, is_without_rowid_table_sql)
- Extensive new oracle tests: CTE queries, CASE WHEN typeof, set operations,
  GROUP_CONCAT, deep subquery nesting, UPDATE with complex SET, aggregate
  FILTER clause, window functions, correlated subqueries, DISTINCT+ORDER BY

Collation-Aware DISTINCT and Sorting (VDBE):
- Propagate per-column collation info (e.g. NOCASE) into SorterOpen P4 string
  using ORDER|COLL1,COLL2,... format
- SorterCursor carries collations vec; all comparison functions accept collation
- New cmp_values_collated() for NOCASE text comparison
- GROUP BY comparisons also collation-aware

VDBE Register Safety:
- get_reg()/set_reg() use usize::try_from() instead of raw casts, handling
  negative indices safely (return Null instead of panicking)
- Add reg_with_offset(), collect_reg_range(), take_reg() helpers
- MakeRecord, ResultRow, Move, Function opcodes refactored to use safe helpers

Parser Correctness:
- parse_first_statement_with_tail() rejects adjacent statements without
  semicolon separator
- INSERT column-list parsing recognizes VALUES keyword to avoid misparse
- Negative float constant-folding narrowed to only -9223372036854775808 (i64::MIN)

SQLite Behavioral Conformance:
- abs() for text now casts to REAL (matching SQLite); remove coerce_numeric()
- FTS5 stemmer: is_ascii_lowercase() guard prevents stripping non-lowercase chars
- JSONB validation uses strict length equality (rejects trailing garbage)
- Record parsing uses checked arithmetic to prevent integer overflow

Concurrency/MVCC Fixes:
- Fix sheaf conformal version disagreement logic (&& -> ||)
- WAL offset allocation moved inside commit-page-index lock for physical/logical
  order consistency
- 2PC abort rejects MarkerWritten and Committing states (not just Committed)
- Rowid allocator: replace end field with remaining counter using wrapping_add

Index Regen Fixes:
- Blob concatenation support in sqlite_concat
- Large-shift edge case returns -1 for negative operands (matches SQLite)
- Scalar min/max propagate NULL immediately
- RTRIM collation only trims ASCII space (0x20)

Infrastructure:
- SlidingWindowHistogram tracks per-slot max values
- VFS SHM race condition fix: ref increment under VFS lock (memory + windows)
- WAL checksum avoids temp buffer copy; frame count overflow guard (u32::MAX)
- Freelist ptrmap_entry_offset handles pending-byte page in pointer-map groups
- B-tree cursor tests for interior separator cell traversal
- Vectorized scan: SelectionIndexOutOfBounds error with column context
- C-API: sqlite3_close uses close_in_place, retains handle on error
- Function parity matrix expanded to 194 tests (window functions, stateful meta)
- New behavioral quirk suite and verification script

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13","title":"T6.7.13 Conflict-topology engineering for page-level MVCC: contention-aware layout, hot-page deflection, and adaptive fill-factor control","description":"Once hybrid fallback and hidden serialization are removed, the real remaining asymptote for FrankenSQLite's page-level MVCC is page conflict rate itself. If too many independent writers still land on the same physical pages in the same transaction window, the engine will continue to pay abort/retry and tail-latency costs even after planner/runtime honesty, publication-plane work, and group-commit improvements land.

This bead adds a new promised-land subtrack: conflict-topology engineering. The core idea is to stop treating page conflicts as an unfortunate output of the storage layout and start treating them as something the engine can observe, model, and reshape. The engine should learn a conflict heat map / overlap graph from real commit behavior, adapt allocator and split policy to reduce future writer overlap, tune fill factor where it materially affects contention, and detect pathological hot pages early enough to deflect or recluster them online.

This is not license for semantic drift, fragile heuristics, or opaque self-modification. The work must remain bounded, observable, reversible, and explainable. The goal is a storage engine whose concurrency unit is not only MVCC-protected but actively engineered for lower collision rates under real workloads.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The engine can observe and quantify writer-overlap topology on the real file-backed path and use that signal in at least one evidence-backed placement or mitigation policy.
2. At least one contention-aware layout/control policy materially reduces conflict/abort pressure, writer overlap, or p95/p99 write latency on representative and adversarial workloads without semantic drift.
3. Any online hotspot mitigation is bounded, observable, reversible, and covered by deterministic fault/recovery tests; it may not silently destabilize data layout or introduce hidden blocking.
4. Every child bead in this subtree includes comprehensive unit/property tests, deterministic e2e replay scripts, detailed structured logging, artifact hashes, and first-failure diagnostics.
5. The subtree preserves concurrent-writer default behavior, B-Tree correctness, crash/recovery semantics, and backend-identity clarity.","notes":"Execution split:
- `.13.1`: conflict heat telemetry, overlap graph, and operator diagnostics.
- `.13.2`: contention-aware allocator, split policy, and adaptive fill-factor control.
- `.13.3`: online hot-page deflection / reclustering for pathological hotspots.
- `.13.4`: certification suite with adversarial hotspot workloads and replayable evidence.

Future-self rationale:
- This subtrack attacks the actual concurrency asymptote after real-path honesty: page conflict rate.
- It is accretive to the existing promised-land work rather than replacing it. Planner/runtime honesty, publication planes, and commit service policy make the system worth optimizing; conflict-topology engineering makes the concurrency substrate itself more favorable.
- Treat `.13.2` and `.13.3` as destination slices; `.13.1` is the required observability runway and `.13.4` is the proof gate.

User-facing intent:
- Higher write concurrency on real workloads.
- Lower abort/retry pressure on hot tables and indexes.
- Better p95/p99 latency under bursty multi-writer load.
- More stable behavior as the database grows and hotspots emerge.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":900,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:13.111004328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:13.543439111Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-uplift","architecture-audit-2026-03","btree","conflict-topology","mvcc","next-gen-architecture","optimization","performance","promised-land","storage-wiring","track6","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:13.111004328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.1","title":"T6.7.13.1 Conflict-heat telemetry, page-overlap graph, and operator diagnostics","description":"Instrument the real file-backed path to build a deterministic conflict heat map and overlap graph for writer activity at the page and B-Tree level. This includes capturing which pages co-occur in conflicting or near-conflicting transactions, which tables/indexes accumulate contention heat, and which structural events (splits, merges, page reuse, fill-factor exhaustion) correlate with future overlap.

The purpose of this bead is not generic observability. It is to produce a stable conflict-topology signal that later beads can act on without guessing. The metrics must distinguish real writer overlap from noise introduced by hybrid fallback or exceptional recovery paths, and they must be stable enough to drive deterministic tests and scenario IDs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The runtime emits a deterministic conflict-topology signal for real file-backed workloads, including page-local heat, writer-overlap estimates, and B-Tree/index-local hotspot summaries.
2. Unit/property tests cover telemetry correctness, stable bucketing/aggregation rules, scenario-ID determinism, and protection against silent schema/log-field drift.
3. Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_conflict_heat.sh` to run representative hotspot workloads with fixed seeds and emit a machine-readable summary plus artifact hashes.
4. Structured logs emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `btree_id`, `table_or_index_role`, `page_no`, `commit_seq`, `writer_overlap_estimate`, `conflict_heat`, `split_pressure`, `allocation_class`, `elapsed_ns`, and first-failure diagnostics.
5. The telemetry is precise enough that later placement/deflection beads can use it without re-deriving conflict topology from raw traces.","notes":"Verification blueprint:
- Unit/property layer:
  - Cover deterministic aggregation, heat-decay or rolling-window rules if present, page-role attribution, and invariants for overlap-graph construction.
- E2E/replay layer:
  - `scripts/verify_t6_7_conflict_heat.sh` should include at least append-hotspot, queue-table, monotone-counter, and secondary-index-hotspot scenarios with fixed seeds.
- Logging contract:
  - Include enough fields to explain why a page became hot, not just that it did.
- User-facing intent:
  - Operators and future implementers should be able to see where concurrency is being lost before policy work begins.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:13.915601401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:17.362033367Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-uplift","architecture-audit-2026-03","btree","conflict-topology","mvcc","optimization","performance","storage-wiring","track6","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:16.975692069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:13.915601401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:17.361981961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.2","title":"T6.7.13.2 Contention-aware allocator, split policy, and adaptive fill-factor control","description":"Use the conflict-topology signal to drive contention-aware page placement: allocator choice, split policy, page reuse class, and adaptive fill-factor control. The goal is to reduce future writer overlap by making page layout less collision-prone under the workloads the engine actually sees.

This bead is where conflict-topology engineering becomes operational. It should not be a bag of heuristics glued onto the allocator. Policies must be explicit, bounded, explainable, and reversible. The engine should be able to say why a page was placed or split a certain way, which conflict signal informed that choice, and what safety limits prevented overreaction.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The allocator/split path can consume conflict-topology signals to choose among explicit placement, split-shape, reuse, or fill-factor policies, and the policy can run in advisory/shadow mode before enforcement.
2. An operator-visible override or kill switch can revert to the baseline placement policy without semantic drift, and rollout thresholds are explicit rather than heuristic folklore.
3. Deterministic unit/property tests cover B-Tree invariants, row ordering, uniqueness, visibility, crash/recovery equivalence, policy stability under repeated hotspot patterns, and advisory-vs-enforced mode consistency.
4. Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_topology_allocator.sh` to run representative and adversarial workloads with fixed seeds and compare conflict rate, abort rate, throughput, and tail latency against a non-topology-aware baseline.
5. Structured logs emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `policy_id`, `policy_mode`, `placement_policy`, `split_reason`, `fill_factor`, `page_role`, `predicted_overlap_delta`, `observed_overlap_delta`, `abort_rate`, `latency_p95_ns`, `operator_override_active`, and first-failure diagnostics.
6. The policy reduces meaningful conflict pressure on at least one important workload family without semantic drift, hidden serialization, or unexplainable mode changes.","notes":"Verification blueprint:
- Unit/property layer:
  - Cover sequential inserts, random inserts, mixed read/write churn, hot counters, queue-table patterns, and secondary-index hot spots.
  - Validate that topology-aware placement never violates core B-Tree invariants or persistence semantics.
  - Prove advisory/shadow mode and enforced mode share the same semantics; they may differ only in whether the policy is enacted.
- E2E/replay layer:
  - `scripts/verify_t6_7_topology_allocator.sh` must emit before/after artifact hashes, fixed-seed replay commands, and an explicit shadow-vs-enforced comparison.
- Logging contract:
  - Every placement decision should be diagnosable after the fact with both predicted and observed impact fields, plus whether an operator override or rollout threshold forced baseline behavior.
- Rollout discipline:
  - This bead must encode a safe path from observe -> advise -> enforce, with clear rollback to baseline.
- User-facing intent:
  - Reduce writer collisions by design, not just after-the-fact retry handling.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":420,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:14.655240658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:29:29.278282977Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-uplift","architecture-audit-2026-03","btree","conflict-topology","mvcc","next-gen-architecture","optimization","performance","promised-land","storage-wiring","track6","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:14.655240658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:17.737524900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.3","title":"T6.7.13.3 Online hot-page deflection, reclustering, and bounded hotspot escape hatches","description":"Add an online escape hatch for pathological hotspots: bounded hot-page deflection or reclustering that can redistribute the worst collision zones without requiring offline rebuilds. This may include directed page deflection, targeted reclustering, side-split style mitigation, or another evidence-backed online strategy, but it must remain bounded and explainable.

This bead is intentionally ambitious. It is the step that says FrankenSQLite should not merely measure or bias away from future conflicts; it should be able to intervene when the workload has already produced a pathological hotspot. That intervention must preserve semantics, durability, and concurrent-writer guarantees under live load.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The engine can identify pathological hotspot pages or regions and trigger an explicit bounded mitigation path with clear eligibility, budget, rollout, and rollback/disable rules.
2. Deterministic unit/property tests cover online movement/reclustering safety, lost-update avoidance, crash/restart equivalence, stale-reader handling via published snapshot generations, and rollback/disable behavior.
3. Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_hot_page_deflection.sh` to exercise adversarial hotspot scenarios under concurrent readers/writers with fixed seeds, failure injection, replayable artifacts, and explicit publication-generation checks.
4. Structured logs emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `hot_page_id`, `mitigation_policy`, `trigger_reason`, `heat_before`, `heat_after`, `budget_ns`, `budget_pages`, `publication_generation`, `migration_outcome`, `rollback_reason`, `operator_override_active`, and first-failure diagnostics.
5. The mitigation materially improves at least one adversarial hotspot workload without regressing correctness, durability, snapshot visibility, or concurrent-writer default behavior.","notes":"Verification blueprint:
- Unit/property layer:
  - Cover bounded intervention budgets, page movement invariants, read visibility during intervention, published-snapshot generation changes, and disable/rollback semantics.
- E2E/replay layer:
  - `scripts/verify_t6_7_hot_page_deflection.sh` should include a monotone-counter hotspot, queue-head hotspot, and secondary-index skew hotspot with fault injection and replayable generation traces.
- Logging contract:
  - Operators must be able to see not only that mitigation occurred, but why it triggered, which publication generation it coordinated through, what budget it consumed, and whether it helped.
- User-facing intent:
  - Pathological hotspots should not permanently pin the system to poor concurrency behavior.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":420,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:15.423563932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:29:41.543958515Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-uplift","architecture-audit-2026-03","btree","conflict-topology","mvcc","next-gen-architecture","optimization","performance","promised-land","storage-wiring","track6","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:15.423563932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:18.115556069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:29:41.543887402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:18.500601461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

### Deliverable: Reusable prepared DML plan objects with semantic parity guards

### What Was Implemented

#### 1. Pre-compiled INSERT VdbePrograms at prepare() time (`compile_and_wrap`)

Modified `Connection::compile_and_wrap()` to pre-compile simple VALUES-based INSERT statements into real VdbePrograms at `prepare()` time, instead of storing placeholder programs. The pre-compiled path sets `db: Some(...)` and `pager: Some(...)` on the `PreparedStatement`, enabling the reuse fast path.

**Criteria for pre-compilation:**
- `InsertSource::Values(_)` — direct VALUES clause
- `InsertSource::DefaultValues` — DEFAULT VALUES
- `InsertSource::Select(sel)` where `sel.body.select` is `SelectCore::Values(_)` and no compounds

Complex `INSERT...SELECT` (with real SELECT sources) still gets a placeholder program and falls back to full `execute_statement` dispatch.

UPDATE and DELETE statements still use placeholder programs + full dispatch because their execution pipelines have complex materialization logic (LIMIT scoping, trigger row collection) not yet safely cacheable.

#### 2. `execute_prepared_insert_reuse()` fast path with autocommit wrapping

When `execute_prepared_with_params` detects a pre-compiled INSERT (`stmt.db.is_some()`), it calls `execute_prepared_insert_reuse()` which:

1. **Autocommit wrapping**: Calls `ensure_autocommit_txn()` / `resolve_autocommit_txn()` to ensure a pager transaction is active (matching `execute_statement` behavior)
2. **Statement savepoint**: Uses `with_internal_statement_savepoint()` for INSERT within explicit transactions
3. **Trigger support**: Fires BEFORE/AFTER INSERT triggers when present
4. **FK enforcement**: Calls `enforce_fk_on_insert()` when foreign keys are enabled
5. **Autoincrement**: Refreshes sqlite_sequence cache after insert
6. **Affected-row accounting**: Sets `last_changes` correctly

The inner execution (`execute_prepared_insert_inner`) calls `execute_table_program` with the cached VdbeProgram, completely skipping codegen on each execution.

#### 3. Semantic Parity Guards

- Schema cookie validation (`ensure_schema_unchanged`) runs before every execution — DDL changes invalidate the prepared statement
- Cross-connection rejection (ptr equality check) prevents misuse
- Statement savepoint protection within explicit transactions matches `execute_statement` behavior
- The `test_prepared_insert_reuse_semantic_parity_with_execute` test directly validates that prepared INSERT produces identical results to `Connection::execute` for the same data

### Tests (6 new, all passing)

1. `test_prepared_insert_reuse_multiple_executions` — 5 sequential executions with different params, verifies `stmt.db.is_some()` (pre-compiled), correct data
2. `test_prepared_insert_reuse_semantic_parity_with_execute` — side-by-side comparison of prepared INSERT vs direct execute, identical results
3. `test_prepared_insert_reuse_within_explicit_transaction` — BEGIN/COMMIT wrapping with pre-compiled INSERT
4. `test_prepared_insert_reuse_rollback_in_explicit_transaction` — ROLLBACK correctly undoes prepared INSERT writes
5. `test_prepared_insert_reuse_or_ignore` — INSERT OR IGNORE with pre-compiled program silently skips duplicates
6. `test_prepared_insert_select_uses_fallback` — INSERT...SELECT correctly gets `db=None` (placeholder) and falls back to full dispatch

### Validation

- `cargo test -p fsqlite-core --lib` => 1531 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored
- `cargo clippy -p fsqlite -p fsqlite-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` => PASS (exit 0)
- `cargo fmt --all --check` => PASS

### Files Changed

1. **`crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`**:
   - `compile_and_wrap()`: Split INSERT from UPDATE/DELETE; pre-compile simple VALUES INSERTs with real VdbeProgram
   - `execute_prepared_insert_reuse()`: Added autocommit + statement savepoint wrapping
   - `execute_prepared_insert_inner()`: Extracted inner execution logic (triggers, FK, program execution)
   - Added 6 new tests in `pager_routing_tests` module

### CREATE TABLE (§12.5)
`CREATE [TEMP] TABLE [IF NOT EXISTS] [schema.]name (column-def [, constraint]*) [WITHOUT ROWID] [STRICT]`.
Also: `CREATE TABLE ... AS select-stmt`.

**Column definition:** `name [type] [constraint]*`. Column constraints: PRIMARY KEY [ASC|DESC] [conflict] [AUTOINCREMENT], NOT NULL [conflict], UNIQUE [conflict], CHECK (expr), DEFAULT (expr|literal|number), COLLATE, REFERENCES (foreign key), [GENERATED ALWAYS] AS (expr) [STORED|VIRTUAL].

**Table constraints:** PRIMARY KEY (cols) [conflict], UNIQUE (cols) [conflict], CHECK (expr), FOREIGN KEY (cols) REFERENCES table [(cols)] [foreign-key-clause].

**Type affinity rules** (first match wins): 1. Contains "INT" → INTEGER. 2. Contains "CHAR"/"CLOB"/"TEXT" → TEXT. 3. Contains "BLOB" or empty → BLOB. 4. Contains "REAL"/"FLOA"/"DOUB" → REAL. 5. Otherwise → NUMERIC.

**WITHOUT ROWID:** Index B-tree clustered on PK. Requires explicit PK. No rowid pseudo-column, no AUTOINCREMENT, INTEGER PK ≠ rowid alias.

**STRICT (3.37+):** Column types restricted to INT/INTEGER/REAL/TEXT/BLOB/ANY. Type checking enforced on INSERT/UPDATE.

**Generated columns (3.31+):** VIRTUAL (computed on read, not stored, can't index directly). STORED (computed on write, stored, can be indexed). Cannot reference later generated columns.

**AUTOINCREMENT:** Only on INTEGER PRIMARY KEY. Uses sqlite_sequence table. Guarantees rowids never reused.

**Foreign key clause:** REFERENCES parent (col) [ON DELETE/UPDATE {SET NULL|SET DEFAULT|CASCADE|RESTRICT|NO ACTION}] [MATCH {SIMPLE|PARTIAL|FULL}] [[NOT] DEFERRABLE [INITIALLY DEFERRED|IMMEDIATE]]. SQLite parses MATCH but enforces only MATCH SIMPLE. Requires `PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON`.

### CREATE INDEX (§12.6)
`CREATE [UNIQUE] INDEX [IF NOT EXISTS] [schema.]name ON table (indexed-column [,...]) [WHERE expr]`.

**Partial indexes:** WHERE clause restricts rows. Planner uses only when query WHERE implies index WHERE.
**Expression indexes:** Index on computed expressions. Planner matches via AST structural equality after normalization.

### CREATE VIEW (§12.7)
`CREATE [TEMP] VIEW [IF NOT EXISTS] [schema.]name [(aliases)] AS select`. Expanded inline (not materialized). Read-only unless INSTEAD OF trigger defined.

### CREATE TRIGGER (§12.8)
`CREATE [TEMP] TRIGGER [IF NOT EXISTS] [schema.]name {BEFORE|AFTER|INSTEAD OF} {DELETE|INSERT|UPDATE [OF col,...]} ON table [FOR EACH ROW] [WHEN expr] BEGIN stmt; ... END`.

**Timing:** BEFORE (can modify/prevent), AFTER (post-DML), INSTEAD OF (views only).
**Pseudo-tables:** INSERT: NEW only. DELETE: OLD only. UPDATE: both OLD and NEW.
**WHEN clause:** Trigger body executes only if WHEN is true.
**Body:** Multiple DML statements. Can reference OLD, NEW, RAISE().
**Recursive triggers:** `PRAGMA recursive_triggers=ON`. Max depth SQLITE_MAX_TRIGGER_DEPTH (1000).

**Rust safety directive (CRITICAL):** Trigger execution MUST NOT use call-stack recursion. MUST use explicit heap-allocated frame stack (`Vec<VdbeFrame>`). MUST enforce capability-budgeted memory ceiling via Cx for nested frames.

### Other DDL (§12.9)
**ALTER TABLE:** RENAME TO, RENAME COLUMN, ADD COLUMN, DROP COLUMN (3.35+, always rewrites table).
**DROP:** TABLE, INDEX, VIEW, TRIGGER — all with IF EXISTS.

SQL text -> Lexer (memchr-accelerated, zero-copy spans) -> Parser (recursive descent, Pratt precedence) -> AST (strongly typed) -> Name Resolution (table/column binding, * expansion) -> Query Planning (index selection, cost, join ordering) -> VDBE Bytecode Generation (190+ opcodes) -> Execution (fetch-execute, match dispatch) -> Results (iterator of Row).

## §10.1 Lexer Detail

~150 TokenType variants. Each token carries TokenType + Span (byte range + line/col).

**Token categories:** Literals (Integer, Float, String, Blob, Variable), Identifiers (Id, QuotedId with DQS flag), Keywords (~120 KwXxx variants), Operators/punctuation (Plus through Concat), Special (Eof, Error).

**QuotedId note:** "hello" is ALWAYS QuotedId at lexer level (matching C SQLite tokenize.c:413). DQS legacy behavior (reinterpreting as string literal) handled in name resolution. QuotedId tokens carry EP_DblQuoted flag.

**Operator note:** Eq/EqEq and Ne/LtGt preserved as distinct tokens for diagnostics and pretty-printing, but parser treats each pair identically.

**Literal parsing:** String: single-quoted, '' escape, memchr-accelerated. Numbers: decimal, hex (0x prefix), float (.e/E). Blob: X'hex', must have even digit count. Error tokens for invalid input (unterminated string, bad hex, unrecognized char).

**Line/column tracking:** Maintains line/col counters. Tokens carry Span with byte offsets + (line, col) at start.

## §10.2 Parser Detail

Hand-written recursive descent (NOT generated parser). C SQLite's parse.y (~1,900 productions, ~76KB) as authoritative grammar reference. Deliberate switch from Lemon LALR(1) for Rust ergonomics, error recovery, debuggability.

**Structure:** One method per grammar production. Key methods: parse_statement -> parse_select_stmt (with/core/compound/order/limit) -> parse_insert_stmt (upsert/returning) -> parse_update_stmt -> parse_delete_stmt -> parse_create_table_stmt (column_def/constraint) -> parse_create_index/view/trigger -> parse_drop/alter -> parse_begin/commit/rollback -> parse_pragma -> parse_explain -> parse_expr (Pratt).

**Pratt precedence table (11 levels):**
1. OR | 2. AND | 3. NOT (prefix) | 4. =,==,!=,<>,IS,IN,LIKE,GLOB,BETWEEN,MATCH,REGEXP,ISNULL,NOTNULL | 5. <,<=,>,>= | 6. &,|,<<,>> | 7. +,- | 8. *,/,% | 9. || (concat), ->, ->> | 10. COLLATE | 11. ~ (bitwise not), unary +/-

Key: equality/membership (4) and relational (5) at SEPARATE levels — `a = b < c` parses as `a = (b < c)`.

**ESCAPE note:** Not in Pratt dispatch table. Parsed as optional suffix of LIKE/GLOB handler.

**Error recovery:** Record error (token, expected, span) -> skip to sync point (semicolon/EOF/statement keyword) -> continue parsing -> return all errors with partial AST.

## §10.3 AST Node Types

**Statement enum:** Select, Insert, Update, Delete, CreateTable/Index/View/Trigger/VirtualTable, Drop, AlterTable, Begin, Commit, Rollback, Savepoint, Release, Attach, Detach, Pragma, Vacuum, Reindex, Analyze, Explain.

**SelectStatement:** with, body (SelectBody = SelectCore + compounds), order_by, limit.

**SelectCore enum:** Select { distinct, columns, from, where, group_by, having, windows } | Values(Vec<Vec<Expr>>) — VALUES is first-class construct.

**Expr enum (~15+ variants):** Literal, Column, BinaryOp, UnaryOp, Between, In, Like (with escape), Case, Cast, Exists, Subquery, FunctionCall (with filter + over), Collate, IsNull, Raise, JsonAccess, RowValue, Placeholder. All carry Span.

 {"id":"bd-1xds","title":"§17.3 Deterministic Concurrency Tests: Lab Runtime + FsLab Harness","description":"E2E Test:
- test_e2e_bd_1xds_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.

## SUMMARY
Implements deterministic concurrency testing using asupersync's lab runtime via the FsLab harness (§4.2.3). All MVCC tests run under deterministic scheduling with fixed seeds for exact reproducibility. The harness supports fault injection via FaultInjectingVfs, deterministic repro artifacts on failure, seed taxonomy (schedule/entropy/fault/fuzz seeds derived from root seed), and CI runs each concurrency test with 100 different seeds. Failing seeds produce self-contained repro bundles for one-command reproduction.

## KEY DATA STRUCTURES / ALGORITHMS
- **FsLab:** `fsqlite_harness::lab::FsLab` wrapper around asupersync's lab runtime. Configured with seed, worker_count, and max_steps. Provides deterministic async task scheduling.
- **Seed Management:** Fixed seed per test for reproducibility. CI runs 100 different seeds per test. Failing seed recorded in test failure message.
- **Seed Taxonomy:** Root test_seed derives: schedule_seed, entropy_seed, fault_seed, fuzz_seed via `H(test_seed || purpose_tag || scope_id)` where H is xxh3_64 or SplitMix64 and purpose_tag is ASCII ("schedule", "entropy", "fault", "fuzz").
- **FaultInjectingVfs:** `fsqlite_harness::vfs::FaultInjectingVfs` wraps UnixVfs. Supports `FaultSpec::partial_write("test.db-wal").at_offset_bytes(4096).bytes_written(2048).after_count(50)` style injection.
- **Repro Artifacts:** On failure with `ASUPERSYNC_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR` set, emit: `repro_manifest.json`, `event_log.txt`, `failed_assertions.json`, optional `trace.async` and `inputs.bin`.
- **repro_manifest.json Schema:** schema_version, test_id, seed, scenario_id, config_hash, trace_fingerprint, input_digest, oracle_violations, passed.
- **Replay Workflow:** Load manifest, re-run with `ASUPERSYNC_SEED=<seed>`, if trace.async exists replay directly, divergence produces divergence artifact.
- **Oracle Report:** `report.oracle_report.all_passed()` assertion validates all MVCC invariants held.

## NORMATIVE INVARIANTS
- INV-DET-1: Every concurrency test MUST use a fixed seed for exact reproducibility.
- INV-DET-2: CI MUST run each concurrency test with 100 different seeds.
- INV-DET-3: Failing seed MUST be recorded in the test failure message.
- INV-DET-4: When ASUPERSYNC_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR is set, any failing run MUST emit a self-contained repro bundle.
- INV-DET-5: Seed derivation MUST follow `H(test_seed || purpose_tag || scope_id)` with stable hash function.
- INV-DET-6: Replay with same seed and scenario MUST produce identical execution trace (determinism guarantee).
- INV-DET-7: repro_manifest.json MUST conform to the minimum schema (schema_version, test_id, seed, scenario_id, config_hash, trace_fingerprint, oracle_violations, passed).

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS
- `test_mvcc_two_writers_different_pages`: T1 inserts rowids 1-100, T2 inserts rowids 1001-1100 concurrently. Both commit. Final count = 200. Uses seed 0xDEADBEEF, 4 workers, 200K max steps.
- `test_mvcc_two_writers_same_page_conflict`: Two transactions write to same page range. One must abort or both succeed if cells are disjoint (per merge ladder §5.10).
- `test_deterministic_replay_same_seed`: Run same scenario twice with identical seed. Verify execution traces are identical.
- `test_fault_injection_partial_wal_write`: Inject partial write at WAL offset 4096 after 50 writes. Verify database recovers correctly on next open.
- `test_repro_artifact_generation`: Set ASUPERSYNC_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR, run a test that fails by assertion. Verify repro_manifest.json, event_log.txt, and failed_assertions.json are emitted with correct schema.
- `test_seed_taxonomy_derivation`: Derive schedule_seed, entropy_seed, fault_seed, fuzz_seed from root seed. Verify deterministic and distinct.
- `test_ci_100_seeds_coverage`: Run a concurrency scenario with seeds 0..99. Verify all complete (pass or fail) and any failure records its seed.
- `test_fault_injection_io_error_on_read`: Inject I/O read failure, verify transaction aborts cleanly without corruption.
- `test_replay_divergence_detection`: Replay with tampered trace.async file, verify divergence artifact emitted at first mismatch.

- test_e2e_bd_1xds_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.
Run the full MVCC deterministic concurrency test suite under FsLab. Execute each test with 100 seeds in CI. Verify zero non-determinism: same seed always produces same outcome. Inject a deliberate fault (partial WAL write), verify recovery. Trigger a failing test, verify repro artifacts are emitted in the correct directory layout. Replay the failing test from the repro manifest and verify identical failure.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- FsLab harness operational: deterministic task scheduling with configurable workers and max_steps.
- All MVCC concurrency tests pass under deterministic scheduling with fixed seeds.
- FaultInjectingVfs supports partial writes, I/O errors, and configurable fault timing.
- CI runs 100 seeds per concurrency test; failing seeds recorded.
- Repro artifacts emitted on failure with correct schema and directory layout.
- Replay from repro manifest produces identical execution (no flakes).
- Seed taxonomy follows normative derivation rule.

### Spec Content (Lines 16403-16510)

All MVCC tests run under asupersync's lab runtime via fsqlite-harness's FsLab wrapper (Section 4.2.3).

**Setup pattern:** Create FsLab with fixed seed (e.g., 0xDEADBEEF), worker_count(4), max_steps(200_000). Run async closure that opens in-memory database, creates table, spawns concurrent writer tasks (e.g., "writer.low" and "writer.high"), executes transactions, awaits completion, verifies results via query. Assert oracle_report.all_passed().

**Seed management:** Each test uses a fixed seed for reproducibility. CI runs each concurrency test with 100 different seeds. A failing seed is recorded in the test failure message for exact replay.

**Deterministic repro artifacts (asupersync-native):**
When ASUPERSYNC_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR is set, failing deterministic lab runs MUST emit self-contained repro bundles:
- Directory layout: {test_id}/repro_manifest.json, event_log.txt, failed_assertions.json, trace.async (optional), inputs.bin (optional)
- Seed taxonomy: test_seed (root), derived seeds: schedule_seed, entropy_seed, fault_seed, fuzz_seed
- Derivation rule: derived = H(test_seed || purpose_tag || scope_id) where H is xxh3_64 or SplitMix64
- repro_manifest.json minimum schema: schema_version, test_id, seed, scenario_id, config_hash, trace_fingerprint, input_digest, oracle_violations, passed

**Replay workflow:** Load repro_manifest.json, re-run with ASUPERSYNC_SEED=<seed>, replay trace.async if exists (divergence produces divergence artifact).

**Fault injection:** Lab reactor supports injecting I/O failures:
- FaultInjectingVfs wrapping UnixVfs
- FaultSpec::partial_write("test.db-wal").at_offset_bytes(4096).bytes_written(2048).after_count(50)

### Unit Tests Required
1. test_lab_two_writers_different_pages: Two concurrent writers inserting into different rowid ranges, both commit, total count = 200
2. test_lab_fixed_seed_reproducibility: Same seed produces identical execution trace across 10 runs
3. test_lab_100_seeds_ci: Run two-writer test with 100 different seeds, all pass
4. test_lab_failing_seed_recorded: Intentionally failing test records failing seed in error message
5. test_lab_repro_manifest_schema: Failing test emits repro_manifest.json with all required fields (schema_version, test_id, seed, scenario_id, config_hash, trace_fingerprint, oracle_violations, passed)
6. test_lab_event_log_emitted: Failing test emits event_log.txt
7. test_lab_failed_assertions_json: Failing test emits failed_assertions.json
8. test_lab_seed_derivation_xxh3: Derived seeds use H(test_seed || purpose_tag || scope_id) with xxh3_64
9. test_lab_seed_taxonomy: Repro manifest records schedule_seed, entropy_seed, fault_seed, fuzz_seed
10. test_lab_replay_from_manifest: Load repro_manifest.json and re-run with ASUPERSYNC_SEED produces identical failure
11. test_lab_trace_replay_divergence: Replay with modified code produces divergence artifact with first mismatched event
12. test_lab_fault_injection_partial_write: FaultInjectingVfs partial_write at offset, database recovers correctly
13. test_lab_fault_injection_after_count: Fault triggers after N operations, not before
14. test_lab_worker_count_4: Lab runs with worker_count(4) and max_steps(200_000) without exceeding step budget

### E2E Test
End-to-end validation: Run a complex MVCC scenario (100 threads inserting into separate rowid ranges) under FsLab deterministic scheduling. Execute with 100 different seeds via CI. For any failing seed, verify repro bundle is emitted with complete repro_manifest.json (seed taxonomy, oracle_violations, config_hash). Replay the failing seed from the manifest and confirm identical failure. Inject I/O faults (partial writes to WAL) and verify crash recovery under deterministic scheduling. Verify that trace fingerprints are stable across replays with the same seed.

 {"id":"bd-1xfri","title":"S3-FIFO Buffer Pool Eviction (§15.1)","description":"Replace current buffer pool eviction with S3-FIFO (Yang et al. 2023), the state-of-the-art eviction algorithm. Score 8.0 in the cross-project scoreboard. S3-FIFO consistently beats LRU, LRU-K, ARC, and LIRS across diverse workloads while being simpler and more cache-friendly.

## Background
Buffer pool eviction determines which pages stay in memory when the pool is full. LRU (Least Recently Used) is the textbook approach but suffers from scan pollution (a single sequential scan evicts all hot pages) and poor performance on skewed access patterns. ARC (Adaptive Replacement Cache) improves on LRU but requires per-access metadata updates that contend under concurrency.

## Algorithm: S3-FIFO
Three queues:
1. SMALL queue (10% of total capacity): FIFO admission filter. New pages enter here.
2. MAIN queue (90% of capacity): FIFO with frequency-based reinsertion. Pages promoted from SMALL if accessed again.
3. GHOST queue (tracks recently evicted pages): No data, just page IDs. Used for frequency estimation.

Eviction logic:
- Evict from SMALL first (FIFO order). If evicted page was accessed while in SMALL, promote to MAIN instead.
- If SMALL is empty, evict from MAIN (FIFO order). If evicted page was accessed while in MAIN, reinsert at tail.
- Posterior-guided adaptive split: adjust the SMALL/MAIN ratio based on observed hit rates (Bayesian update on which queue contributes more hits).

## Why S3-FIFO
- Lock-free friendly: FIFO queues only touch head/tail, not middle (unlike LRU which moves accessed items)
- Scan resistant: sequential scans fill SMALL queue and get evicted without polluting MAIN
- Adaptive: posterior-guided split handles workload changes

## Key Paper
Yang, Yue, Rashmi, et al. 2023: 'FIFO Queues are All You Need for Cache Eviction'

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 3-queue S3-FIFO structure implemented
- [ ] Posterior-guided adaptive queue split
- [ ] Lock-free or low-contention implementation
- [ ] Hit rate improvement over LRU on mixed workloads
- [ ] Scan resistance demonstrated
- [ ] Benchmark: S3-FIFO vs LRU vs ARC hit rates on trace-driven workload

## Success Criteria

+//! Canonical built-in function parity matrix for bd-2yqp6.5.1.
+//!
+//! This module provides a machine-readable contract that links the declared
+//! built-in-function parity surface from [`crate::parity_taxonomy`] to the
+//! concrete differential/unit evidence that currently validates each function
+//! family. The matrix is intentionally feature-centric:
+//!
+//! - every built-in taxonomy feature must appear at least once,
+//! - every supported variant must point at explicit verification evidence,
+//! - unsupported variants must carry rationale,
+//! - known rusqlite binding differences are documented separately from real
+//!   parity gaps.
+
+use std::collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet};
+
+use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
+
+use crate::parity_taxonomy::{FeatureCategory, FeatureId, ParityStatus, build_canonical_universe};
+
+/// Bead identifier for log correlation and evidence indexing.
+pub const BEAD_ID: &str = "bd-2yqp6.5.1";
+
+/// Schema version for forward-compatible consumers.
+pub const MATRIX_SCHEMA_VERSION: u32 = 1;
+
+const TARGET_SQLITE_VERSION: &str = "3.52.0";
+
+/// High-level built-in function family for grouping and reporting.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub enum FunctionFamily {
+    /// Scalar functions, including planner hints.
+    Scalar,
+    /// Aggregate functions.
+    Aggregate,
+    /// Window functions.
+    Window,
+    /// Date/time functions.
+    Datetime,
+    /// Math functions.
+    Math,
+    /// Stateful/meta helpers exposed as functions.
+    Meta,
+}
+
+impl FunctionFamily {
+    /// Stable display label.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub const fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
+        match self {
+            Self::Scalar => "scalar",
+            Self::Aggregate => "aggregate",
+            Self::Window => "window",
+            Self::Datetime => "datetime",
+            Self::Math => "math",
+            Self::Meta => "meta",
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+/// Primary verification strategy for an evidence link.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub enum VerificationOracle {
+    /// Direct oracle comparison against rusqlite / C SQLite behaviour.
+    RusqliteDifferential,
+    /// Verification against mathematically-known expected values.
+    MathematicalOracle,
+    /// Engine-level tests executing SQL through `fsqlite-core::Connection`.
+    EngineUnit,
+    /// Library-level unit tests on built-in implementation modules.
+    LibraryUnit,
+    /// Metadata/state assertions that do not compare full SQL output sets.
+    MetadataAssertion,
+}
+
+impl VerificationOracle {
+    /// Stable display label.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub const fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
+        match self {
+            Self::RusqliteDifferential => "rusqlite-differential",
+            Self::MathematicalOracle => "mathematical-oracle",
+            Self::EngineUnit => "engine-unit",
+            Self::LibraryUnit => "library-unit",
+            Self::MetadataAssertion => "metadata-assertion",
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+/// Verification result currently recorded for a function variant.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub enum VerificationStatus {
+    /// Evidence currently passes.
+    Passing,
+    /// Evidence passes except for documented rusqlite binding artefacts.
+    KnownBindingDifference,
+    /// Differential evidence currently fails and the parity gap is documented.
+    Failing,
+    /// Variant is intentionally unsupported.
+    Unsupported,
+}
+
+impl VerificationStatus {
+    /// Stable display label.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub const fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
+        match self {
+            Self::Passing => "passing",
+            Self::KnownBindingDifference => "known-binding-difference",
+            Self::Failing => "failing",
+            Self::Unsupported => "unsupported",
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+/// Link to a concrete verification artifact.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct CoverageLink {
+    /// Relative workspace path to the test file.
+    pub test_file: String,
+    /// Concrete test function name.
+    pub test_name: String,
+    /// Primary verification strategy used by that test.
+    pub oracle: VerificationOracle,
+}
+
+/// Machine-readable row for a built-in function feature/variant pair.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct BuiltinFunctionVariant {
+    /// Stable row ID (`BFUNC-###`).
+    pub id: String,
+    /// Canonical feature identifier from the parity taxonomy.
+    pub feature_id: FeatureId,
+    /// Human-readable feature title from the parity taxonomy.
+    pub feature_title: String,
+    /// Declared feature parity status from the taxonomy.
+    pub declared_status: ParityStatus,
+    /// Function family.
+    pub family: FunctionFamily,
+    /// Concrete variant label within the feature family.
+    pub variant_name: String,
+    /// Current verification result.
+    pub verification_status: VerificationStatus,
+    /// Representative SQL statements or probes for this variant.
+    pub representative_sql: Vec<String>,
+    /// Concrete evidence links.
+    pub coverage_links: Vec<CoverageLink>,
+    /// Human-readable notes for reviewers.
+    pub notes: String,
+    /// Rationale for unsupported or otherwise special variants.
+    pub rationale: Option<String>,
+    /// Cross-cutting tags.
+    pub tags: BTreeSet<String>,
+}
+
+/// Deterministic coverage summary for reporting.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct MatrixSummary {
+    /// Total number of rows in the matrix.
+    pub total_variants: usize,
+    /// Number of distinct taxonomy features represented.
+    pub total_features: usize,
+    /// Counts grouped by family.
+    pub variants_by_family: BTreeMap<String, usize>,
+    /// Counts grouped by verification status.
+    pub variants_by_status: BTreeMap<String, usize>,
+}
+
+/// The canonical built-in function parity matrix.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct BuiltinFunctionParityMatrix {
+    /// Schema version.
+    pub schema_version: u32,
+    /// Bead identifier.
+    pub bead_id: String,
+    /// Target SQLite version.
+    pub target_sqlite_version: String,
+    /// Rows keyed by stable row ID for deterministic iteration.
+    pub variants: BTreeMap<String, BuiltinFunctionVariant>,
+}
+
+impl BuiltinFunctionParityMatrix {
+    /// Build the canonical matrix.
+    #[must_use]
+    #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]
+    pub fn canonical() -> Self {
+        let feature_catalog = builtin_feature_catalog();
+        let mut variants = BTreeMap::new();
+        let mut seq: u16 = 0;
+
+        let mut add = |family: FunctionFamily,
+                       feature_title: &str,
+                       variant_name: &str,
+                       verification_status: VerificationStatus,
+                       representative_sql: &[&str],
+                       coverage_links_seed: &[(&str, &str, VerificationOracle)],
+                       notes: &str,
+                       rationale: Option<&str>,
+                       tags: &[&str]| {
+            let (feature_id, declared_status) = feature_catalog
+                .get(feature_title)
+                .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("unknown built-in feature title: {feature_title}"))
+                .clone();
+            seq += 1;
+            let id = format!("BFUNC-{seq:03}");

+    /// Validate structural and coverage invariants.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn validate(&self) -> Vec<String> {
+        let mut violations = Vec::new();
+        let feature_catalog = builtin_feature_catalog();
+        let mut covered = BTreeSet::new();
+
+        for variant in self.variants.values() {
+            covered.insert(variant.feature_id.clone());
+
+            if variant.feature_title.is_empty() {
+                violations.push(format!("{} has empty feature_title", variant.id));
+            }
+            if variant.variant_name.is_empty() {
+                violations.push(format!("{} has empty variant_name", variant.id));
+            }
+            if let Some((expected_id, expected_status)) =
+                feature_catalog.get(&variant.feature_title)
+            {
+                if expected_id != &variant.feature_id {
+                    violations.push(format!(
+                        "{} feature/title mismatch: expected {} for {}",
+                        variant.id, expected_id, variant.feature_title
+                    ));
+                }
+                if *expected_status != variant.declared_status {
+                    violations.push(format!(
+                        "{} declared status mismatch for {}",
+                        variant.id, variant.feature_title
+                    ));
+                }
+            } else {
+                violations.push(format!(
+                    "{} references unknown built-in feature title {}",
+                    variant.id, variant.feature_title
+                ));
+            }
+
+            match variant.verification_status {
+                VerificationStatus::Passing => {
+                    if variant.coverage_links.is_empty() {
+                        violations.push(format!(
+                            "{} is {:?} but has no coverage links",
+                            variant.id, variant.verification_status
+                        ));
+                    }
+                    if variant.representative_sql.is_empty() {
+                        violations.push(format!(
+                            "{} is {:?} but has no representative SQL",
+                            variant.id, variant.verification_status
+                        ));
+                    }
+                }
+                VerificationStatus::KnownBindingDifference | VerificationStatus::Failing => {
+                    if variant.coverage_links.is_empty() {
+                        violations.push(format!(
+                            "{} is {:?} but has no coverage links",
+                            variant.id, variant.verification_status
+                        ));
+                    }
+                    if variant.representative_sql.is_empty() {
+                        violations.push(format!(
+                            "{} is {:?} but has no representative SQL",
+                            variant.id, variant.verification_status
+                        ));
+                    }
+                    if variant.rationale.is_none() {
+                        violations.push(format!(
+                            "{} is {:?} but lacks rationale",
+                            variant.id, variant.verification_status
+                        ));
+                    }
+                }
+                VerificationStatus::Unsupported => {
+                    if variant.rationale.is_none() {
+                        violations
+                            .push(format!("{} is unsupported but lacks rationale", variant.id));
+                    }
+                }
+            }
+
+            for link in &variant.coverage_links {
+                if link.test_file.is_empty() || link.test_name.is_empty() {
+                    violations.push(format!(
+                        "{} contains incomplete coverage link {:?}",
+                        variant.id, link
+                    ));
+                }
+            }
+        }
+
+        for (title, (feature_id, _status)) in &feature_catalog {
+            if !covered.contains(feature_id) {
+                violations.push(format!(
+                    "builtin feature {title} ({feature_id}) missing from parity matrix"
+                ));
+            }
+        }
+
+        violations
+    }
+
+    /// Return rows sorted deterministically by row ID.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn sorted_variants(&self) -> Vec<&BuiltinFunctionVariant> {
+        self.variants.values().collect()
+    }
+
+    /// Return rows for a given function family.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn variants_by_family(&self, family: FunctionFamily) -> Vec<&BuiltinFunctionVariant> {
+        self.variants
+            .values()
+            .filter(|variant| variant.family == family)
+            .collect()
+    }
+
+    /// Compute a deterministic summary.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn summary(&self) -> MatrixSummary {
+        let mut variants_by_family = BTreeMap::new();
+        let mut variants_by_status = BTreeMap::new();
+        let mut features = BTreeSet::new();
+
+        for variant in self.variants.values() {
+            features.insert(variant.feature_id.clone());
+            *variants_by_family
+                .entry(variant.family.as_str().to_owned())
+                .or_insert(0) += 1;
+            *variants_by_status
+                .entry(variant.verification_status.as_str().to_owned())
+                .or_insert(0) += 1;
+        }
+
+        MatrixSummary {
+            total_variants: self.variants.len(),
+            total_features: features.len(),
+            variants_by_family,
+            variants_by_status,
+        }
+    }
+
+    /// Serialize to deterministic JSON.
+    ///
+    /// # Errors
+    ///
+    /// Returns an error when serialization fails.
+    pub fn to_json(&self) -> Result<String, serde_json::Error> {
+        serde_json::to_string_pretty(self)
+    }
+}
+
+fn builtin_feature_catalog() -> BTreeMap<String, (FeatureId, ParityStatus)> {
+    let universe = build_canonical_universe();
+    let mut features = BTreeMap::new();
+    for feature in universe.features_by_category(FeatureCategory::BuiltinFunctions) {
+        features.insert(feature.title.clone(), (feature.id.clone(), feature.status));
+    }
+    features
+}
+
+fn coverage_links(seed: &[(&str, &str, VerificationOracle)]) -> Vec<CoverageLink> {
+    seed.iter()
+        .map(|(test_file, test_name, oracle)| CoverageLink {
+            test_file: (*test_file).to_owned(),
+            test_name: (*test_name).to_owned(),
+            oracle: *oracle,
+        })
+        .collect()
+}

+fn canonical_sql_statement(sql: &str) -> String {
+    let trimmed = sql.trim();
+    if trimmed.ends_with(';') {
+        trimmed.to_owned()
+    } else {

+    }

+fn render_repro_sql_script(
+    scenario: &QuirkScenario,
+    envelope: &ExecutionEnvelope,
+    reduction: Option<&MismatchReduction>,
+) -> String {
+    let feature_ids = feature_ids_for_titles(scenario.feature_titles);
+    let workload = reduction
+        .map(|value| &value.minimized_envelope.workload)
+        .unwrap_or(&envelope.workload);
+
+    let mut content = format!(
+        "-- bead_id={BEAD_ID}\n\
+         -- fixture_id={fixture_id}\n\
+         -- corpus_scenario_id={corpus_scenario_id}\n\
+         -- category={category}\n\
+         -- feature_ids={feature_ids}\n\
+         -- replay_command={REPLAY_COMMAND}\n\n",
+        fixture_id = scenario.fixture.id,
+        corpus_scenario_id = scenario.corpus_scenario_id,
+        category = scenario.category,
+        feature_ids = feature_ids.join(","),
+    );
+
+    for statement in workload {
+        content.push_str(&canonical_sql_statement(statement));
+        content.push('\n');
+    }
+
+    content
+}

+fn write_divergence_artifacts(
+    scenario: &QuirkScenario,
+    envelope: &ExecutionEnvelope,
+    result: &DifferentialResult,
+    reduction: Option<&MismatchReduction>,
+) -> Result<(PathBuf, PathBuf), String> {
+    let artifact_dir = workspace_root()
+        .join("artifacts")
+        .join(BEAD_ID)
+        .join("minimal-repros");
+    fs::create_dir_all(&artifact_dir).map_err(|error| {
+        format!(
+            "create_dir_failed path={} error={error}",
+            artifact_dir.display()
+        )
+    })?;
+
+    let source_bytes = scenario_source_bytes(scenario)?;
+    let source_sha256 = sha256_hex(&source_bytes);
+    let feature_ids = feature_ids_for_titles(scenario.feature_titles);
+    let envelope_hash = &result.artifact_hashes.envelope_id[..16];
+
+    let repro_content = render_repro_sql_script(scenario, envelope, reduction);
+    let repro_sha256 = sha256_hex(repro_content.as_bytes());
+    let repro_path = artifact_dir.join(format!("{}-{envelope_hash}.sql", scenario.id));
+    fs::write(&repro_path, repro_content.as_bytes()).map_err(|error| {
+        format!(
+            "repro_write_failed path={} error={error}",
+            repro_path.display()
+        )
+    })?;
+
+    let reduction_payload = reduction.map(|minimized| {
+        json!({
+            "original_workload_len": minimized.original_workload_len,
+            "minimized_workload_len": minimized.minimized_workload_len,
+            "removed_workload_indices": minimized.removed_workload_indices,
+            "reduction_ratio": minimized.reduction_ratio(),
+            "minimized_envelope": minimized.minimized_envelope,
+            "minimized_result": minimized.minimized_result,
+        })
+    });
+
+    let source_label = scenario
+        .fixture_path
+        .as_ref()
+        .map(|path| path.display().to_string())
+        .unwrap_or_else(|| format!("inline://{}", scenario.fixture.id));
+    let artifact_path = artifact_dir.join(format!("{}-{envelope_hash}.json", scenario.id));
+    let payload = json!({
+        "bead_id": BEAD_ID,
+        "fixture_id": scenario.fixture.id,
+        "category": scenario.category,
+        "corpus_scenario_id": scenario.corpus_scenario_id,
+        "feature_titles": scenario.feature_titles,
+        "feature_ids": feature_ids,
+        "fixture_source": {
+            "label": source_label,
+            "sha256": source_sha256,
+        },
+        "replay_command": REPLAY_COMMAND,
+        "trace_id": result.metadata.trace_id,
+        "run_id": result.metadata.run_id,
+        "scenario_id": result.metadata.scenario_id,
+        "seed": result.metadata.seed,
+        "envelope": envelope,
+        "differential_result": result,
+        "minimal_reduction": reduction_payload,
+        "minimal_repro_sql": {
+            "path": repro_path.display().to_string(),
+            "sha256": repro_sha256,
+        },
+    });
+    let content = serde_json::to_vec_pretty(&payload)
+        .map_err(|error| format!("artifact_serialize_failed: {error}"))?;
+    fs::write(&artifact_path, content).map_err(|error| {
+        format!(
+            "artifact_write_failed path={} error={error}",
+            artifact_path.display()
+        )
+    })?;
+
+    Ok((artifact_path, repro_path))
+}

+fn scenario_inventory_maps_to_feature_ids_and_corpus_scenarios() {
+    let scenarios = quirk_scenarios();
+    let mut ids = BTreeSet::new();
+    let mut corpus_ids = BTreeSet::new();
+    let mut categories = BTreeSet::new();
+
+    for scenario in &scenarios {
+        assert!(
+            ids.insert(scenario.id.clone()),
+            "duplicate scenario id {}",
+            scenario.id
+        );
+        assert!(
+            corpus_ids.insert(scenario.corpus_scenario_id),
+            "duplicate corpus scenario id {}",
+            scenario.corpus_scenario_id
+        );
+        assert!(
+            !feature_ids_for_titles(scenario.feature_titles).is_empty(),
+            "scenario {} must resolve feature ids",
+            scenario.id
+        );
+        assert!(
+            !fixture_sql_ops(&scenario.fixture).is_empty(),
+            "scenario {} must have replayable SQL",
+            scenario.id
+        );
+        categories.insert(scenario.category);
+    }
+
+    assert!(categories.contains("affinity"));
+    assert!(categories.contains("collation"));
+    assert!(categories.contains("null"));
+    assert!(categories.contains("overflow"));
+}

+        let report = run_suite(&sqlite3_path, &fixtures, mode).unwrap_or_else(|error| {
+            panic!("bead_id={BEAD_ID} mode={mode:?} run_suite failed: {error}")
+        });
+        assert!(
+            report.all_passed(),
+            "bead_id={BEAD_ID} mode={mode:?} oracle mismatch: failed={} diffs={:?}",
+            report.failed,
+            report
+                .reports
+                .iter()
+                .filter(|fixture| !fixture.passed)
+                .flat_map(|fixture| fixture.diffs.iter())
+                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
+        );
+    }
+}

+#[test]
+fn supported_behavioral_quirks_match_csqlite_differentially() {

+    let mut pass_count = 0_usize;
+    let mut divergences = Vec::new();
+
+    for (index, scenario) in scenarios.iter().enumerate() {
+        let envelope = envelope_for_scenario(scenario, index);
+        let fsqlite = FsqliteExecutor::open_in_memory()
+            .unwrap_or_else(|error| panic!("bead_id={BEAD_ID} failed to open fsqlite: {error}"));
+        let csqlite = CsqliteExecutor::open_in_memory()
+            .unwrap_or_else(|error| panic!("bead_id={BEAD_ID} failed to open csqlite: {error}"));
+        let result = run_differential(&envelope, &fsqlite, &csqlite);
+
+        if matches!(result.outcome, Outcome::Pass) {
+            pass_count += 1;
+            continue;
+        }
+
+        let reduction = minimize_mismatch_workload(
+            &envelope,
+            FsqliteExecutor::open_in_memory,
+            CsqliteExecutor::open_in_memory,
+        )
+        .unwrap_or_else(|error| {
+            panic!(
+                "bead_id={BEAD_ID} scenario={} minimization failed: {error}",
+                scenario.id

+        let (artifact_path, repro_path) =

+                .unwrap_or_else(|error| {
+                    panic!(
+                        "bead_id={BEAD_ID} scenario={} artifact write failed: {error}",
+                        scenario.id
+                    )
+                });
+
+        divergences.push(format!(
+            "scenario={} corpus_scenario_id={} feature_ids={:?} outcome={} artifact={} repro={}",

+            artifact_path.display(),
+            repro_path.display()

+        pass_count + divergences.len(),
+        scenarios.len(),
+        "bead_id={BEAD_ID} missing scenario outcomes"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        divergences.is_empty(),
+        "bead_id={BEAD_ID} behavioral quirk mismatches:\n{}",
+        divergences.join("\n")
+    );
+
+    eprintln!(
+        "INFO bead_id={BEAD_ID} case=behavioral_quirk_summary passes={} divergences={} replay='{}'",
+        pass_count,
+        divergences.len(),
+        REPLAY_COMMAND
+    );
+}

+#[test]
+fn repro_script_uses_minimized_workload_when_present() {

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Verification gate for bd-2yqp6.5.3:
+# SQLite behavioral quirks (affinity/collation/NULL/overflow) differential suite.
+#
+# Deterministic replay:
+#   bash scripts/verify_bd_2yqp6_5_3_behavioral_quirks.sh
+#
+# Structured logging contract:
+#   emits JSONL events with trace_id/run_id/scenario_id/seed/timing/outcome
+#   to artifacts/bd-2yqp6.5.3/<run_id>/events.jsonl
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+BEAD_ID="bd-2yqp6.5.3"
+SCENARIO_ID="PARITY-QUIRK-E3"
+SEED="${SEED:-3530}"
+TIMESTAMP_UTC="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
+RUN_ID="${BEAD_ID}-${TIMESTAMP_UTC}-${SEED}"
+TRACE_ID="trace-${RUN_ID}"
+ARTIFACT_DIR="artifacts/${BEAD_ID}/${RUN_ID}"
+EVENTS_JSONL="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/events.jsonl"
+TEST_LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/test.log"
+REPORT_JSON="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/report.json"
+
+mkdir -p "${ARTIFACT_DIR}"
+start_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+
+emit_event() {
+  local phase="$1"
+  local event_type="$2"
+  local outcome="$3"
+  local message="$4"
+  local now_ns elapsed_ms
+  now_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+  elapsed_ms="$(( (now_ns - start_ns) / 1000000 ))"
+  printf '{"trace_id":"%s","run_id":"%s","scenario_id":"%s","seed":%d,"phase":"%s","event_type":"%s","outcome":"%s","elapsed_ms":%d,"timestamp":"%s","message":"%s"}\n' \
+    "${TRACE_ID}" "${RUN_ID}" "${SCENARIO_ID}" "${SEED}" "${phase}" "${event_type}" "${outcome}" "${elapsed_ms}" "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" "${message}" \
+    >> "${EVENTS_JSONL}"
+}
+
+run_gate() {
+  local phase="$1"
+  shift
+  emit_event "${phase}" "start" "running" "running: $*"
+  if "$@" 2>&1 | tee -a "${TEST_LOG}"; then
+    emit_event "${phase}" "pass" "pass" "command passed"
+    return 0
+  fi
+  emit_event "${phase}" "fail" "fail" "command failed"
+  return 1
+}
+
+echo "=== ${BEAD_ID}: behavioral quirk verification ==="
+echo "run_id=${RUN_ID}"
+echo "trace_id=${TRACE_ID}"
+echo "scenario_id=${SCENARIO_ID}"
+
+emit_event "bootstrap" "start" "running" "verification started"
+
+RESULT="pass"
+
+if ! run_gate "behavioral_quirk_suite" \
+  rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_2yqp6_5_3_behavioral_quirk_suite -- --nocapture; then
+  RESULT="fail"
+fi
+
+TEST_LOG_SHA256="$(sha256sum "${TEST_LOG}" | awk '{print $1}')"
+EVENTS_SHA256="$(sha256sum "${EVENTS_JSONL}" | awk '{print $1}')"
+
+cat > "${REPORT_JSON}" <<EOF_JSON
+{
+  "trace_id": "${TRACE_ID}",
+  "run_id": "${RUN_ID}",
+  "scenario_id": "${SCENARIO_ID}",
+  "seed": ${SEED},
+  "bead_id": "${BEAD_ID}",
+  "commands": [
+    "rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_2yqp6_5_3_behavioral_quirk_suite -- --nocapture"
+  ],
+  "artifacts": {
+    "events_jsonl": "${EVENTS_JSONL}",
+    "events_sha256": "${EVENTS_SHA256}",
+    "test_log": "${TEST_LOG}",
+    "test_log_sha256": "${TEST_LOG_SHA256}"
+  },
+  "result": "${RESULT}"
+}
+EOF_JSON
+
+emit_event "finalize" "info" "${RESULT}" "report written to ${REPORT_JSON}"
+
+if [[ "${RESULT}" != "pass" ]]; then
+  echo "[GATE FAIL] ${BEAD_ID} behavioral quirk verification failed"
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+echo "[GATE PASS] ${BEAD_ID} behavioral quirk verification passed"
+echo "Artifacts: ${ARTIFACT_DIR}"
